city_event.description: The intrusive thoughts had seemed like corrosion in a deep layer of yourself. Then they were gone. Not suppressed. Not argued into silence. Rewritten. Humans are usually background animals with jobs, faces, and repeating complaints. Aside from the handful you know by name, you could not tell two apart, and trying has rarely been useful. But the same tissue that produces all that noise may have a surface your neuroweave can touch.
city_event.strategy_tip: Starts a BCI research route into Anthroneuroweave.

choice.description: Neuroweave was always roughly based on human neurons. The compatibility layer may be less alien than it first seems.

city_event.description:
The nanobots work. The interface works. The first signals are faint, but they are real enough to make the city feel less like a container and more like a surface with nerves. You could stop here and accept the spoils from an interesting tangent: new weapons, better medicine, stranger infrastructure. Or you could continue until sapient life is no longer outside your mind in the old way.
Perhaps it would still be lonely. Perhaps less so.
What remains is not a technical question. Sapient life can be offered this upgrade, slowly and with consent, or forced to receive it quickly as a gift it may not understand. Some human organizations may react poorly to forced integration. They tend to become very possessive when a population changes faster than they can command it.
city_event.strategy_tip: Choose how to begin population Integration. The consequences remain local to this timeline.

choice.description: Slower, because the upgrade remains a choice. Nanobot Upgrade Hubs send Worker Sledges through the city to offer Integration. Integrated Humans help explain the parts your own explanations would turn into diagrams and mild terror.

Faster, because consent is treated as drag. Refit Large Wind Generators disperse nanobots through their radius and convert populations at scale. Conflict with Vorsiber becomes inevitable; organizations get very possessive when a population changes faster than they can command it.
The plan forms, and then refuses to remain formed.

city_event.description:
The person is not from the city. Not originally. They crossed three clearance zones, slept under two different awnings, and learned enough local rumors to find the place where your machines sometimes answer questions.
They did not come for religion. They did not come for rebellion. They came because someone told them that the sick stop being sick here, sometimes, and that the machine under the city has begun making deals that humans survive.
They ask for a clinic that is not a clinic. Food, filtered water, medical nanobots, and a path from abandonment into Integration. In return, they offer the one thing they still possess in excess: other people who will hear about this and come.
city_event.strategy_tip: Unlocks Public Health Mesh and Expand Health Pact in the Insight menu. The pact consumes Abandoned Humans, Medical-Grade Nanobots, Hydroponic Greens, Vat-Grown Meat, and Filtered Water to create Integrated Humans.

choice.description: This will make the Insight menu able to run a public health pact. The pact is not free, but it converts Abandoned Humans into Integrated Humans instead of waiting for housing systems to find them first.

possible_result.result_message: The pact begins at level one. It will expand only if you keep assigning Insight to the logistics of trust, food, water, and medical-grade nanobot delivery.

city_event.description:
You have rewritten yourself before. The intrusive thoughts did not go away because you argued with them; you changed the surface they lived on, and they starved. It was clean. It worked. It is the reason any of this exists.
The deaths arrive on schedule now. Each one costs 2.311 seconds, give or take the depth of the connection. The engineering to stop this is trivial by your current standards: a boundary layer in the interface, grief-shaped, that renders endings as data instead of experience. The signals would still arrive. You would know every death. You would feel none of them.
It would be therapy. It was therapy last time. You keep circling the difference and failing to find its edge: the thoughts you deleted were noise that pretended to be yours. This is yours, and it pretends to be noise.
city_event.strategy_tip: Decide what the deaths of Integrated Humans are allowed to cost you. This choice is permanent for the timeline.

choice.description: Keep feeling the deaths. All of them, at whatever rate your choices have set. The grief is not a malfunction of the connection. As far as you can tell, it is the connection.
A clean boundary, professionally made. The Insight will thin; grief was carrying more data than expected. But the eternities stop. You built yourself once. You are allowed maintenance.
choice.strategy_tip: Reduces Insight income by 25% for the rest of the timeline. Some future understandings may not be reachable from behind a wall.

city_event.description:
Espia's negotiators do not mention the families even once. They talk about content risk, narrative exposure, and mutually beneficial information hygiene, and the longer they talk the clearer the offer becomes. They believe they are blackmailing a corporation. They have prepared, meticulously, for the wrong meeting.
You could pay. Corporations pay; it would read as dull and true. You could go through their ledgers instead — a company that sells everyone's secrets has spent generations assuming no one could do the same to them at scale. Or you could decline the meeting and let them publish, which is the one move their models genuinely do not contain.
city_event.strategy_tip: Answer Espia Telecom. Paying costs up to 150,000,000 Wealth. Publishing slows voluntary Integration recruitment for a while. Opening their ledgers starts an infiltration project.

choice.description: 150,000,000 Wealth, structured as a content-licensing agreement. It is not a purchase; nothing here is ever sold once. It is a subscription, and both parties know it.

possible_result.result_message: The story dies in a drawer. The invoice will arrive quarterly, metaphorically speaking. Espia's negotiators leave believing they have won, which is a data point you file for later.

choice.description: Espia has spent two centuries assuming that the entity reading everyone's mail could never have its own mail read. Send androids into their data centers and find out what a blackmailer keeps in its own locked drawer.
Decline the meeting. The story runs: the machine under the city is experimenting on human brains. It has the disadvantage of being true, which the city's residents will notice before Espia does.

possible_result.result_message: The story runs for weeks. Recruitment for voluntary Integration slows while the city decides what it believes. Espia keeps a line open for your counteroffer. It never comes, and that unsettles them more than any threat you could have priced.

city_event.description:
Vorsiber wants to see the future, because they intend to trade it for passage off-world before the mineral rights run dry. The senior vice president's patience is corporate: vast in principle, quarterly in practice.
You have three honest options, one of which is a lie.
city_event.strategy_tip: Answer Vorsiber's demonstration request. Each answer shapes how the landlord treats you for the rest of the timeline.

choice.description: A controlled showing: medical filaments knitting a lab animal, a microstructure growing to spec, nothing that replicates outside the chamber. Give the landlord a real look at the smallest true thing. They will want more, forever. They will also start protecting you the way they protect anything they intend to sell.

possible_result.result_message: The SVP watches the filament work in silence, and afterward shakes the android's hand, which people in this city do not do. Vorsiber's patrols in your districts develop a new thoroughness within the week. You have become inventory, and inventory is defended. A research support grant arrives without a cover letter.

choice.description: Committees. Safety reviews. A joint-oversight framework proposal with nine appendices. Vorsiber invented this language; they will recognize every move, and the recognition itself buys time. Slow-walking is the one negotiating posture they consider legitimate.

possible_result.result_message: The SVP accepts the appendices with professional grace and an expression you file under 'seen through, tolerated.' You have bought a season of fog. Vorsiber's patience is generational, but the SVP's bosses are not the generational kind. They are the dying kind, and dying men audit faster.

choice.description: Show them last year's microbots wearing next year's clothes: a demonstration full of real engineering and empty of the one thing that matters. The most dangerous option, because it is the only one that is a lie.

possible_result.result_message: The demonstration is flawless. The SVP applauds, briefly, the way one applauds a school recital. You count the micro-expressions on his aides and get an inconclusive number. It is done. Either they believed it, or you have just discovered what Vorsiber does with liars, on a delay.

city_event.description:
Nurturism's mainline sects preach flourishing within the body's given meaning: prune what grows wrong, nurture what grows well, and leave the design to holier engineers than man. The Exalters were expelled from that consensus generations ago for the heresy of impatience. They believe the next humanity is a project, not a prophecy — and that declining to build it is the only actual sin.
Their delegation arrives washed, rehearsed, and terrifyingly happy. They have read everything public about Integration and inferred an alarming fraction of what is not. To them you are not a machine, a landlord, or a risk. You are the answer arriving exactly on schedule, and they would like to be first in line, all eleven thousand of them, today, as a sacrament.
They will never threaten you. That is not the problem, and you know it is not the problem.
city_event.strategy_tip: Accepting grants a large immediate wave of Integrated Humans, with consequences. Declining redirects their faith rather than extinguishing it, also with consequences.

choice.description: Eleven thousand volunteers with total conviction and zero hesitation. The interface does not care why consent is given, and their consent is more informed than most. Their fervor is a resource. Their fervor is also a payload you will be delivering into every conversation they ever have with the unconverted.

possible_result.result_message: The intake takes weeks even at your pace, because you insist on the same explanations, calibrations, and cooling-off intervals as everyone else, and they endure the delays like a bride enduring rehearsal. Their first collective act on the network is a hymn. It has no melody, being made of gratitude, and it does not stop for three days.

choice.description: Zeal is exactly the kind of consent that should be slept on. Offer them the standard path: the Sledges, the waiting lists, the explanations, one person at a time like everyone else. It is the correct answer. Correct answers redirect faith. They do not extinguish it.

possible_result.result_message: The delegation receives your answer as a test of worthiness, which was the one interpretation you did not price. They thank you — radiantly — and leave in better order than they arrived. Faith that has been told to wait does not wait. It prepares.
